problem:
Do I commit the package-lock.json file created by npm 5?.

npm 5 was released today and one of the new features include deterministic installs with the creation of a package-lock.json file.

Is this file supposed to be kept in source control?

I'm assuming it's similar to yarn.lo
expected_solution_keywords: Do I commit the package-lock.json file created by npm 5?
memory_hint:
Do I commit the package-lock.json file created by npm 5?.

Yes, package-lock.json is intended to be checked into source control. If you're using npm 5+, you may see this notice on the command line: created a lockfile as package-lock.json. You should commit this file. Acco
